title: Data Grid
description: Data Grid renders structured records with DataSource-driven loading, column factories, selection, filtering, sorting, pagination, expandable rows, and virtualization. Use it for operational tables where users need to inspect, refine, and act on larger datasets.
intro: Data Grid is one of the heaviest workhorse components in the system, so the showcase should cover both the simple presets and the more operational states. Start from a clear column model, then layer in selection, filters, pagination, expandable details, and virtualization only when the workflow needs them.

featureSections.title: Basic preset
featureSections.description: Minimal setup with a static data source and text columns. This is the right baseline for read-only operational tables before adding heavier table behaviors.

featureSections.title: Column factory
featureSections.description: Column factories keep common text, number, date, single-select, and multi-select setups shorter and more consistent.

featureSections.title: Selection
featureSections.description: Selection turns the grid from a reporting surface into a bulk-action surface. Use none, single, or multi based on the real action model.

featureSections.title: Server-side features
featureSections.description: Pagination, sorting, and filtering are usually most valuable when the grid is driven by server-side query state rather than a static local array.

featureSections.title: Virtualization
featureSections.description: Virtualization matters when the dataset is large enough that rendering all rows would hurt scroll performance or initial paint.

featureSections.title: Filtering
featureSections.description: Use a mix of text, single-select, multi-select, and date filters when users need to progressively narrow a larger working set.

featureSections.title: Expandable rows
featureSections.description: Expandable rows are useful when the grid should stay compact by default but still reveal secondary metadata without forcing navigation away.

featureSections.title: Full featured
featureSections.description: This shows the component in the denser, more realistic state where selection, sorting, filtering, pagination, and details all coexist.

featureSections.title: Advanced configuration
featureSections.description: Loading states, empty states, default sort, and callback hooks are where the grid begins to feel integrated into a real product workflow.

featureSections.title: Interactive demo
featureSections.description: A grid this central benefits from a playground where selection, layout, filtering, paging, virtualization, and emitted events can all be inspected in one place.

accessibility.title: Grid semantics
accessibility.body:
Treat Data Grid as a high-density interaction surface, not just a styled table. Headers, rows, selection controls, filter fields, expansion toggles, and pagination all contribute to the accessibility model.

| Area | Accessibility behavior |
| --- | --- |
| column headers | expose header semantics and optional sortable interaction |
| selection controls | rely on the checkbox or radio semantics used by the configured selection mode |
| filter inputs | inherit accessible behavior from the embedded field components |
| expandable rows | should expose a clear expand or collapse affordance and state |
| pagination and page size | inherit behavior from pagination and dropdown components |

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior is a combination of normal interactive descendants rather than one custom keystroke model layered over the whole surface.

| Element | Keyboard behavior |
| --- | --- |
| sortable header actions | standard button-like activation behavior |
| filter controls | follow the embedded input, dropdown, or date control behavior |
| selection controls | follow checkbox or radio keyboard behavior |
| expandable toggles | follow standard button behavior |
| pagination controls | follow the shared pagination keyboard model |

accessibility.title: Usage guidance
accessibility.body: Only enable the heavy features the workflow really needs. Dense tables become harder to scan and operate when selection, filtering, sticky headers, expansion, pagination, and virtualization are all turned on without a clear task behind them.

apiSections.title: DataGridComponent - ui-data-grid
apiSections.lead: Config-driven data grid for operational tables. It supports local or remote DataSource loading, selection, filtering, sorting, pagination, expansion, styling variants, and virtualization.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Single source of truth for columns, data loading, selection, filtering, pagination, styling, expansion, virtualization, states, and callbacks.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Defines visible columns and field behavior.
Loads rows from static or server-driven query state.
Enables query refinement and page navigation.
Adds master-detail rows or large-list rendering optimization.

paragraphs: The component is controlled through the config object rather than many small top-level inputs. That makes it easier to keep table state in sync with routing, server queries, or local signals.
For large datasets, virtualization improves paint and scroll behavior, but it should be combined carefully with sticky headers, row expansion, and any feature that changes row height expectations.
blocks.title: Notes

apiSections.title: Callbacks and query flow
apiSections.lead: Most real integrations listen to emitted callbacks and then update query state or UI state outside the grid.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Open details or route to a record page.
Enable bulk actions and summary UI.
Persist filters and show active filter summaries.
Move the result set to another page.
Track detail usage or lazy-load secondary metadata.

paragraphs: A common pattern is to keep query state in a signal, update that signal from callbacks, and rebuild the grid config from the resulting state.
When the DataSource is remote, callbacks should usually feed a query request rather than mutate the currently rendered row array directly.
blocks.title: Notes
